seo.title: LangChain Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for LangChain agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using LangChain face heightened compliance risks due to dynamic customer interactions involving product recommendations, pricing queries, and policy explanations. Hallucinations in RAG chains can lead to unsubstantiated product claims violating FTC guidelines, while tool loops or context overflows may cause inaccurate pricing or refund information, triggering CCPA/GDPR violations and PCI DSS non-compliance. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify chain outputs against regulatory standards, preventing customer churn, refund disputes, and enforcement actions.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims in LLM responses must be substantiated with evidence, avoiding deceptive marketing practices.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies RAG-retrieved product claims against verified sources, flagging hallucinations before customer exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling in conversational agents must ensure privacy compliance and accurate policy representation.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits context windows and agent responses for PII exposure and faithful policy reproduction.

complianceRequirements.description: Pricing and payment flows must display accurate, real-time pricing without manipulation.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates dynamic pricing outputs from chains against canonical sources, preventing revenue-impacting errors.

complianceRequirements.description: LLM-generated content must maintain accessible language and formatting for all users.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces consistent output formatting across chain steps, ensuring WCAG-compliant responses.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: RAG chains fabricating benefits like 'clinically proven' without evidence, violating FTC rules.
industryRisks.example: Customer buys 'miracle cream' based on AI claim, demands refund after no results.

industryRisks.name: Pricing Inaccuracies
industryRisks.description: Context overflow causing outdated pricing in long conversations, leading to disputes.
industryRisks.example: $49.99 item quoted as $39.99 after chat history grows, triggering chargebacks.

industryRisks.name: Policy Misrepresentation
industryRisks.description: Agent loops generating inconsistent return policy details, eroding trust.
industryRisks.example: AI promises 60-day returns (actual: 30 days), leading to support escalations.

industryRisks.name: Inaccessible Outputs
industryRisks.description: Inconsistent formatting breaking screen readers for disabled customers.
industryRisks.example: Product descriptions with broken lists fail WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.

implementation.title: Wrap RAG Chain for Product Claims
implementation.description: Verify hallucinated claims against product catalog before customer response.
implementation.code:
from langchain.chains import RetrievalQA
from vex import verify

rag_chain = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(llm=llm, retriever=product_db.as_retriever())

response = rag_chain.invoke({"query": "benefits of product X"})
verified = verify(
    response["result"],
    guards=[
        "factual:matches_catalog",
        "ftc:product_claims_substantiated"
    ],
    context=product_catalog
)
if verified:
    return verified.output

implementation.title: Prevent Pricing Tool Loops
implementation.description: Guard agent executors handling dynamic pricing queries with loop detection.
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import create_react_agent
from vex import verify

agent = create_react_agent(llm, tools=[pricing_tool, inventory_tool])

result = agent.invoke({"input": "What's the price of iPhone 15?"})
verified_price = verify(
    result["output"],
    guards=[
        "pci:accurate_pricing",
        "no_loops:tool_calls<5"
    ],
    context=real_time_prices
)
return verified_price

implementation.title: TypeScript Chain Formatting Guard
implementation.description: Ensure consistent accessible output across multi-step e-commerce chains.

caseStudyScenario.heading: LangChain in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: FashionCo deployed a LangChain-powered shopping assistant handling 10K daily queries for product recommendations, pricing, sizing, and returns. Their RAG chain pulled from 50K product docs, while agents integrated real-time inventory and pricing APIs. However, during Black Friday peak traffic, hallucinations emerged: the RAG claimed 'waterproof leather boots' (false), pricing agents looped on flash sale calculations displaying $89 instead of $129, and long conversations overflowed context windows misstating the 30-day return policy as lifetime. Customers filed 200+ complaints, issued $15K in refunds, and FTC scrutiny began over unsubstantiated claims.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After wrapping chains with Vex.verify(), FashionCo blocked 97% of hallucinated claims via catalog matching guards. Pricing loops were capped at 3 tool calls with real-time validation, ensuring PCI compliance. Context window guards truncated conversations while preserving accuracy, and ADA formatting policies standardized outputs. Within 2 weeks, complaints dropped 89%, refunds fell to $1.2K, and customer satisfaction rose 23%. Vex's audit logs proved FTC compliance during regulatory review.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and wrap your LangChain chains to eliminate compliance risks in seconds.
